channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Williams sisters earmarked for doubles on Saturday
item.description: Serena Williams' planned appearance alongside older sister Venus in the Wimbledon doubles is added to Saturday's order of play.

item.title: Qualifiers eye more shocks after causing Wimbledon upsets
item.description: Qualifiers Roman Safiullin and Shintaro Mochizuki set their sights on toppling title challengers Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner after causing huge upsets at Wimbledon.

item.title: Djokovic fights off Rinderknech to claim more history as Sinner wins
item.description: Novak Djokovic claims his latest piece of history as he equals Roger Federer's record total of 105 men's singles match wins at Wimbledon by battling past Arthur Rinderknech.

item.title: Sabalenka into round four after comfortable win against Ostapenko
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka remains on course for her first Wimbledon singles title after she beats Latvia's Jelena Ostapenko in straight sets to reach the last 16 at SW19.

item.title: 'You're taking our jobs' - doubles players angry at proposed cuts
item.description: Doubles players are "angry" at proposals to cut their events because it is "taking our jobs away", say Wimbledon champions Julian Cash and Lloyd Glasspool.

item.title: Sabalenka beats Ostapenko to set up blockbuster Osaka meeting
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka battles past Latvia's Jelena Ostapenko to set up a blockbuster fourth-round match against Naomi Osaka at Wimbledon.

item.title: GB's Cash & Glasspool on track for doubles title defence
item.description: British duo Julian Cash and Lloyd Glasspool remain on course to defend their men's doubles title after reaching round three at Wimbledon.

item.title: The French-born star carrying British hopes at Wimbledon
item.description: Arthur Fery is left carrying British hopes at the All England Club after becoming the sole home player to reach the third round.

item.title: 'Not a carnival sideshow' - doubles players fear cutbacks
item.description: A group of leading doubles players accuse the men's ATP Tour of trying to "end doubles as a viable profession".

item.title: Who am I? Guess the tennis star No 4
item.description: Will you guess today's tennis player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Dimitrov beats Mensik to extend emotional Wimbledon return
item.description: Grigor Dimitrov extends his emotional return to Wimbledon after his tearful exit 12 months ago by defeating 15th seed Jakub Mensik to reach the third round.

item.title: Djokovic fends off Rinderknech for record-equalling 105th win
item.description: Novak Djokovic beats Arthur Rinderknech in four sets to go level with Roger Federer for most men's singles wins at SW19, as the seventh seed progresses into the fourth round.

item.title: 'Outstanding' - best shots from day five of Wimbledon
item.description: Enjoy the best shots from day five of Wimbledon 2026, featuring Jelena Ostapenko and Novak Djokovic.

item.title: 'Quite stressed' - Djokovic relieved to get past Rinderknech
item.description: Novak Djokovic says he was dealing with "more tension than usual", but was happy to have got over the line as he beat Arthur Rinderknech in four sets to reach the last 16 at Wimbledon.

item.title: 'Unbelievable' - best shots from day four of Wimbledon
item.description: Enjoy the best shots from day four of Wimbledon 2026, featuring British hopefuls Katie Swan and Arthur Fery.

item.title: Rybakina produces dominant display to progress to third round
item.description: Watch highlights as Elena Rybakina comfortably beats Caty McNally 6-1 6-2 in the second round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Eala's historic Wimbledon run thrills boxing-obsessed Philippines
item.description: The 21-year-old has become the first Filipino to reach the third round of a Grand Slam in the Open era.

item.title: Boulter and Watson among Britons to exit doubles
item.description: Katie Boulter and Heather Watson are among the British players to exit the doubles competition in the first round at Wimbledon on Thursday.

item.title: Swiatek and Rybakina dominate to reach third round
item.description: Iga Swiatek overpowers former finalist Karolina Pliskova as she continues her Wimbledon title defence by reaching the third round in straight sets.

item.title: Fery last Briton standing in Wimbledon singles
item.description: Arthur Fery is the last British player standing in the Wimbledon singles after his compatriots Katie Swan, Jacob Fearnley, and Jan Choinski are beaten.

item.title: How is the Wimbledon schedule decided? Your questions answered
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team answer your questions about the Wimbledon schedule.

item.title: Andreeva in tears after defeat by Krejcikova
item.description: A tearful French Open champion Mirra Andreeva breaks down in tears in her news conference after she is dumped out of Wimbledon by Barbora Krejcikova.

item.title: Vintage Djokovic 'in full flow' to crush Tsitsipas as Sinner wins
item.description: Novak Djokovic outclasses Stefanos Tsitsipas in a statement performance to reach the Wimbledon third round.

item.title: Keys knocks out Swan to end British women's singles hopes
item.description: America's Madison Keys defeats Britain's Katie Swan, the nation's last remaining hope in the women's singles competition, in straight sets. The 26th seed delivers a dominant grass-court display to secure a 6-1, 6-4 victory.

item.title: 'How good is that?' - the best shots from Fery's fightback to reach last 32
item.description: British wildcard Arthur Fery reaches the third round of Wimbledon for the first time in his career, by defeating Otto Virtanen 5-7, 7-6 (7-3), 6-3, 6-3 on day four at Wimbledon.

item.title: 'I just don't want to let people down' - Dimitrov on emotional return to Wimbledon
item.description: Grigor Dimitrov describes the "pure emotion" of playing amongst the "special energy" of a Wimbledon crowd, after the Bulgarian made a triumphant return to SW19 exactly 12 months after injury forced him to retire in the round of 16 in last year's competition.

item.title: Djokovic delivers 'incredible display' to breeze past Tsitsipas
item.description: Novak Djokovic produces a vintage display to beat Stefanos Tsitsipas in straight sets to progress from the second round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: 'A bit of magic!' - best shots from day three at Wimbledon
item.description: Enjoy the best shots from day three of Wimbledon 2026, featuring Coco Gauff and French Open champion Mirra Andreeva.

item.title: Djokovic reflects on 'vintage' performance in win over Tsitsipas
item.description: Noavk Djokovic says he feels privileged to be playing on centre court at 39-years old and doesn't take the opportunity for granted, following his straight sets win over Stefanos Tsitsipas in the second round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: How is Wimbledon tackling climate change?
item.description: Renewable electricity, solar powered water heating and repurposed towels and nets - here's a look at how SW19 aims to become more environmentally sustainable during Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Defending champion Sinner beats Borges to reach round three
item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner overcomes a stern test against Portugal's Nuno Borges on Centre Court, securing a 7-6 (7-4), 7-6 (7-2), 6-4 victory to advance to the third round.

item.title: Williams edged by Joint in epic three‑setter on singles return
item.description: Serena Williams came close to a sensational comeback win on her Wimbledon return before narrowly losing an epic three-setter to Maya Joint 3-6 7-6 (8-6) 3-6 at Wimbledon 2026

item.title: 'I've been dreaming of this moment' - Joint stunned to beat 'legend' Williams
item.description: Maya Joint, 20, says she has been "dreaming" of playing on Centre Court since she was little, after overcoming "legend" Serena Williams to reach the second round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Williams 'takes it all in' on Centre Court return
item.description: Serena Williams receives a standing ovation from the Centre Court crowd as she walks out to play at Wimbledon for the first time in four years.

item.title: The 'bad boy with a good heart' waves farewell at Wimbledon
item.description: Dan Evans bids an emotional farewell at Wimbledon as he and Henry Searle lose in the final match of the Briton's career.

item.title: Williams' knee injury leaves Wimbledon doubles in doubt
item.description: Serena Williams' hopes of playing alongside her older sister Venus in the Wimbledon doubles look in doubt because of a knee injury.

item.title: Kyrgios swears at umpire on Wimbledon return
item.description: Nick Kyrgios swears at an umpire and says he does not care if he is fined as his return to Wimbledon ends in defeat in the men's doubles.

item.title: 120mph serves but movement a struggle - how did Williams' return go?
item.description: Serena Williams loses on her singles return to Wimbledon - but what can we expect next from the tennis legend?

item.title: Eastbourne withdrawal pays off for positive Fearnley
item.description: Jacob Fearnley says his stunning comeback win over Alex Michelsen at Wimbledon justified his decision to pull out of Eastbourne with a back problem.

item.title: Samuel ready to target world's top 100
item.description: British tennis player Toby Samuel says he has got his eyes on the men's top 100 in the world.

item.title: Lobbing Federer changed his life - the cult hero back at Wimbledon
item.description: Marcus Willis is best known for playing Roger Federer on Wimbledon's Centre Court but, 10 years on, he says he is not defined by that magical moment.

item.title: 'Where champions are born' - the making of Jannik Sinner
item.description: Jannik Sinner is the world's best tennis player. This is the journey that forged a champion.

item.title: When five Wimbledon titles in one weekend changed British tennis
item.description: A decade after Andy Murray's 2016 men's singles title, we look at the legacy of the golden weekend where five Wimbledon trophies were won by British players.

item.title: Battling egos and stereotypes - the rise of female tennis coaches
item.description: In a sport like tennis, which prides itself on gender equality, why are there still so few female coaches and what is being done to address the issue?

item.title: Anisimova plots Wimbledon sequel with happier ending
item.description: Many wondered how long it would take Amanda Anisimova to recover from last year's Wimbledon final. About six weeks was the answer.

item.title: Williams is the greatest - but will she produce another great fight?
item.description: Serena Williams will return to the singles court at Wimbledon aged 44 - BBC Sport analyses if she is ready and the challenges she will face.

item.title: How Murray was tempted back to tennis - and would he ever do a Serena?
item.description: British tennis icon Andy Murray discusses returning to coaching with Jack Draper, Serena Williams' comeback and filling the void of not playing.

item.title: 'I never imagined something like this' - Nadal reflects on 2008 Wimbledon win
item.description: Rafael Nadal recalls his seismic five-set victory over Roger Federer in the 2008 Wimbledon final, arguably the greatest men's match ever played.

item.title: What is a 'lucky loser' in tennis?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain how the 'lucky loser' system works in tennis.

item.title: Who has won tennis' 'Battle of the Sexes'?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team details the history of 'Battle of the Sexes' matches in tennis

item.title: What is US Open prize money?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how much prize money is on offer at the US Open.

item.title: Why is there a pineapple on Wimbledon's men's trophy?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at why there is a pineapple on the top of the Wimbledon trophy for the winner of the men's singles.

item.title: Why were there no line judges at Wimbledon 2025?
item.description: Wimbledon are relying on electronic line calling to judge whether a ball is in or out.
